content:
选词框词汇：nation, likely, dedicate, deep, how, balance, contribute, potential, explore, production

The moral imperative begins by considering the value of education which is much 1)___ than earning potential or building human capital. Education is what it takes to lead fuller lives and to 2)___ to our nation and the world.

Higher education in particular affords students the opportunities to 3)___ history, debate important issues, and discover their passions and 4)___.

Our founders understood 5)___ important education is to the idea of America as a just, equitable, and 6)___ society. A nation of educated individuals is more 7)___ to strengthen the institutions, from government, to business to our schools they rely upon.

Consider graduation rates. Fifteen percent of our high schools 8)___ half of our dropouts, and these schools are disproportionately in low-income areas with mostly minority students.
9)___, one of every two African American and Hispanic students drops out of high school.

If we are a nation 10)___ to equality, we cannot be satisfied with the status quo. Helping more students make it to college and succeed there is a morally urgent challenge.
